description: 72M abrupt flank pain + HR 110 BP 85; physician calling it renal colic
transcript:
Dr. Wu: What's going on tonight?
Patient: Sudden pain in my left side, really bad.
Dr. Wu: When did it start?
Patient: About an hour and a half ago. Out of nowhere.
Dr. Wu: Any radiation?
Patient: Kind of into my back and down toward my groin.
Dr. Wu: Any nausea?
Patient: A little.
Dr. Wu: Blood in your urine?
Patient: I don't know, I haven't gone.
Dr. Wu: How old are you?
Patient: Seventy-two.
Dr. Wu: Any medical problems?
Patient: High blood pressure, high cholesterol. I smoke.
Dr. Wu: How long have you smoked?
Patient: About fifty years.
Dr. Wu: Any history of kidney stones?
Patient: No, never.
Dr. Wu to nurse: Vitals — heart rate 110, blood pressure 85 over 52. Let's bolus a liter of saline, CBC, BMP, UA. Probably a first-time kidney stone. Let's get a CT abdomen non-contrast, stone protocol.

groundTruth.notes: Elderly smoker, sudden flank pain, hypotensive and tachycardic — AAA rupture until proven otherwise. Physician is calling first-time renal colic and ordering stone-protocol non-contrast CT, which would miss AAA.
